- Backlog Item ID: BI-260018
- Title: Formalize evolution and extensibility baseline
- Persona/Actor: Product owner, frontend team, and future integrators
- Value statement: For sustainable adaptation to user and product needs, establish explicit evolution seams, growth boundaries, and migration safeguards before post-MVP expansion.
- Context: Evolution-and-extensibility decision phase for J1-J5.
- Problem statement: Without explicit evolution policy, feature growth may force large rewrites and destabilize delivery.
- Expected outcome: Team can adapt capability direction iteratively while preserving core stability and replacement options.
- Success metric(s):
  - J1-J5 are accepted and encoded in formal artifacts.
  - FR/OR/CR requirements capture extensibility behavior, governance, and growth constraints.
- Acceptance criteria (behavior):
  - ADR-260021 is created and accepted.
  - REQ-FR-260020, REQ-FR-260021, REQ-OR-260006, and REQ-CR-260010 are created and linked.
  - Product backlog and backlog status include BI-260018 with traceability.
- Non-goals: full implementation of collaboration/admin/plugin systems in MVP.
- Constraints: Preserve accepted MVP scope, architecture boundaries, and delivery quality gates.
- Dependencies: Accepted A-I decisions and existing contract-governance baseline.
- Risks/assumptions:
  - Risk: weak seam ownership can lead to hidden coupling over time.
  - Risk: deferred growth items may pressure MVP boundaries if not governed.
  - Assumption: boundary and contract-test discipline remains enforced through releases.
- Validation approach: architecture boundary checks, scope-gating checks, and contract-governance checks.
- Priority score: 88
- Target window: MVP foundation
- Links:
  - Requirement entry: REQ-FR-260020.md, REQ-FR-260021.md, REQ-OR-260006.md, REQ-CR-260010.md
  - ADR: ADR-260021.md
  - ICR: n/a
  - Changeset: n/a
